title: Oxford Entrance Test
seoDescription: Take a fast Oxford-style reasoning quiz with staged logic, pattern, deduction, number, and visual challenges.
pageTitle: Only 3% Can Pass This Oxford Test
summary: Only 3% can pass this elite reasoning test.

homepage.title: Can You Pass This Oxford Entrance Test?
homepage.summary: Only 3% can pass this elite reasoning test.

infoPanel.title: About This Oxford Test
infoPanel.intro: This Oxford-style quiz is a fast reasoning challenge inspired by the kind of analytical thinking, pattern recognition, deduction, and careful reading often associated with tutorial-style discussion. It is not an official University of Oxford exam, but it is designed to feel sharp, focused, and mentally demanding.

columns.title: How It Works
columns.body: The quiz moves through five escalating stages: Warm Up, Challenge Round, Expert Round, Impossible Round, and Final 3%. Each short stage mixes visual, number, logic, deduction, pattern, attention, and lateral-style reasoning so the challenge feels varied while steadily becoming harder.

columns.title: About Oxford Admissions
columns.body: Oxford undergraduate admissions are competitive and course-specific. Depending on the subject, applicants may be assessed through academic achievement, written work, admissions tests, interviews, references, and personal statements. There is no single universal Oxford entrance exam for every applicant.

infoPanel.footerBody: Every question in this quiz has one correct answer and an explanation. Your score is for entertainment and self-challenge only; it should not be treated as an official admissions result or academic assessment.

landing.challengeText: Most players never reach the final round.

profiles.title: You beat the test.
profiles.copy: Exceptional reasoning across the full challenge. Your strongest area was {stage}.

profiles.title: Elite reasoning.
profiles.copy: You stayed sharp through the harder rounds. Your strongest area was {stage}.

profiles.title: Sharp under pressure.
profiles.copy: Strong performance across the skill rounds, led by {stage}.

profiles.title: Above-average reasoning.
profiles.copy: You showed good instincts, especially in {stage}.

profiles.title: Solid score.
profiles.copy: You handled several rounds well. Your strongest area was {stage}.

profiles.title: Keep sharpening.
profiles.copy: The hardest rounds reward calm pattern memory. Your best area was {stage}.

stageGroups.title: Warm Up

questions.prompt: Which item does not belong?
questions.explanation: Apple, orange, and banana are fruits. Carrot is a vegetable.

questions.prompt: What comes next?
questions.explanation: The numbers double each time: 3, 6, 12, 24, 48.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 90" style="width:340px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="170" y="53" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">3   6   12   24   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Choose the only even number.
questions.explanation: 34 is the only number divisible by 2.

questions.prompt: Which group has the most dots?
questions.explanation: Group C has six dots, more than the other groups.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 360 90" style="width:360px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="180" y="53" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">A: ●●●   B: ●●●●   C: ●●●●●●   D: ●●</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which shape has six sides?
questions.explanation: A hexagon has six sides.

questions.prompt: Which arrow points the other way?
questions.explanation: A, C, and D point up. B points down.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 90" style="width:330px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="165" y="53" text-anchor="middle" font-size="28" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">A ↑     B ↓     C ↑     D ↑</text></svg>

questions.prompt: All members read. A is a member. What must be true?
questions.explanation: If all members read and A is a member, then A reads.

questions.prompt: Which shape is the only outline?
questions.explanation: C is the only outline shape. The others are filled.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 90" style="width:330px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="165" y="53" text-anchor="middle" font-size="32" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">A ●     B ●     C ○     D ●</text></svg>

stageGroups.encouragement: You cleared the warm-up. Next comes the challenge round, where the patterns get quicker.

stageGroups.title: Challenge Round

questions.prompt: Which option completes the size pattern?
questions.explanation: The circles alternate small, large, small, large, then small.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 90" style="width:260px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="130" y="53" text-anchor="middle" font-size="28" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">●  ●   ●  ●   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which image has the odd symbol?
questions.explanation: C contains a star while the others are circles.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 90" style="width:340px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="170" y="53" text-anchor="middle" font-size="28" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">A ●     B ●     C ★     D ●</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which missing shape keeps the pair rule?
questions.explanation: Each pair shows the same shape in black, then white.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 240 90" style="width:240px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="120" y="53" text-anchor="middle" font-size="34" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">● ○     ■ ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which shape should come after two triangles?
questions.explanation: The pattern is circle, triangle, triangle, square, then repeats.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 360 90" style="width:360px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="180" y="53" text-anchor="middle" font-size="28" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">○ △ △ □   ○ △ △ ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which number is the smallest?
questions.explanation: 39 is the smallest of the four numbers.

questions.prompt: How many stars are in the row?
questions.explanation: There are four stars in the row.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 90" style="width:340px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="170" y="53" text-anchor="middle" font-size="26" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">● ★ ● ★ ● ★ ★ ●</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which group is the odd one out?
questions.explanation: A, C, and D each have four dots. B has five.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 400 100" style="width:400px;max-width:100%;height:100px"><text x="200" y="58" text-anchor="middle" font-size="20" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">A: ●●●●   B: ●●●●●   C: ●●●●   D: ●●●●</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which symbol appears twice?
questions.explanation: The triangle is the only symbol shown two times.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" style="width:300px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="150" y="53" text-anchor="middle" font-size="32" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">●   ■   ▲   ◆   ▲</text></svg>

stageGroups.encouragement: Good pace. The expert round now asks for slower, more deliberate thinking.

stageGroups.title: Expert Round

questions.prompt: Which square number comes after 25?
questions.explanation: These are square numbers: 1², 2², 3², 4², 5², then 6².
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 90" style="width:340px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="170" y="53" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">1   4   9   16   25   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: How many dots come next?
questions.explanation: The groups contain 1, 2, 4, then 7 dots. The increases are +1, +2, and +3.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 90" style="width:330px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="165" y="53" text-anchor="middle" font-size="26" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">●     ●●     ●●●●     ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which number is the odd one out?
questions.explanation: 27, 64, and 125 are cubes. 100 is not.

questions.prompt: What number finishes the bottom row?
questions.explanation: In each row, the first two numbers multiply to give the third.
questions.visual:
<svg viewBox="0 0 260 120" style="width:260px;max-width:100%;height:120px"><text x="130" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">2  3  6
3  5  15
4  6  ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which option is the mirror image of the first shape?
questions.explanation: B is the left-to-right mirror image of the first arrow.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 380 90" style="width:380px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="190" y="53" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">Shape →    A →    B ←    C ↑    D ↓</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which position does the star take?
questions.explanation: Counting from the left, the star is in the third position.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" style="width:300px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="150" y="53" text-anchor="middle" font-size="32" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">●   ●   ★   ●   ●</text></svg>

questions.prompt: If ▲ = 3 and ● = 5, what is ▲ + ● + ▲?
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 90" style="width:260px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="130" y="53" text-anchor="middle" font-size="28" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">▲ + ● + ▲ = ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: A box has twice as many red balls as blue balls. There are 6 blue balls. How many balls are there in total?
questions.explanation: There are 12 red balls and 6 blue balls, which gives 18 balls in total.

stageGroups.encouragement: You reached expert territory. The impossible round is built to stop most players.

stageGroups.title: Impossible Round

questions.prompt: Remove every filled circle. How many shapes remain?
questions.explanation: Three outlined shapes remain after the filled circles are removed.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 90" style="width:330px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="165" y="53" text-anchor="middle" font-size="30" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">●   ○   ■   △   ●   ◇</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which option is this shape turned upside down?
questions.explanation: C is the original triangle rotated 180 degrees.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 380 90" style="width:380px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="190" y="53" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">Shape ▲    A ▲    B ▶    C ▼    D ◀</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Every marble in Box A is blue. Some blue marbles are large. Are all marbles in Box A large?
questions.explanation: Some blue marbles being large does not prove all blue marbles are large.

questions.prompt: Only round shapes fit the slot. This shape fits the slot. What must be true?
questions.explanation: If only round shapes fit and this shape fits, then the shape must be round.

questions.prompt: Two numbers add to 10. One number is 4 times the other. What is the smaller number?
questions.explanation: The numbers are 2 and 8. The smaller number is 2.

questions.prompt: You pass the person in second place. What place are you now in?
questions.explanation: Passing the person in second place puts you in second place.

questions.prompt: If the light is on, the door is locked. The door is open. What must be true?
questions.explanation: An open door is not locked, so the light cannot be on.

questions.prompt: If the pattern continues, what is the next shape?
questions.explanation: The cycle is circle, square, triangle, diamond. After triangle comes diamond.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 380 90" style="width:380px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="190" y="53" text-anchor="middle" font-size="30" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">●   ■   ▲   ◆   ●   ■   ▲   ?</text></svg>

stageGroups.encouragement: Final stretch. The last stage is the 3% Oxford challenge.

questions.prompt: What comes next in the sequence?
questions.explanation: This is the look-and-say sequence. 111221 is read as three 1s, two 2s, and one 1.
questions.visual:
<svg viewBox="0 0 260 160" style="width:260px;max-width:100%;height:160px"><text x="130" y="88" text-anchor="middle" font-size="20" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">1
11
21
1211
111221
?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: A clock loses 5 minutes every hour. If it is set correctly at 12:00, what time does it show after 6 real hours?
questions.explanation: The clock loses 30 minutes in 6 hours, so it shows 5:30.

questions.prompt: Which number completes the rule?
questions.explanation: Each bottom number is the sum of the two numbers above it.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 90" style="width:260px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="130" y="53" text-anchor="middle" font-size="30" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">7 + 11 = ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: A, B, and C stand in one row. A is not first. B is immediately left of C. What is the order?
questions.explanation: B must stand directly before C, and A cannot be first. The order is B C A.

questions.prompt: Which conclusion is valid? All L are M. No M are N.
questions.explanation: If all L are M, and no M are N, then no L are N.

questions.prompt: Which value completes the grid?
questions.explanation: In each row, the right number equals left plus middle: 4 + 8 = 12.
questions.visual:
<svg viewBox="0 0 260 120" style="width:260px;max-width:100%;height:120px"><text x="130" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">2  3  5
5  6  11
4  8  ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: A shape turns 90 degrees each step. What comes next?
questions.explanation: The turns are up, right, down, then left.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 90" style="width:260px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="130" y="53" text-anchor="middle" font-size="40" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">↑   →   ↓   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Boxes A, B, and C hold a circle, square, and triangle. A is not circle or triangle. C is not square. What is in Box B?
questions.explanation: A must be square. C cannot be square, so B must be circle.
